theorem: For signal s with replay(s) = s[0:n/2] ⊕ s[0:n/2]: m1(replay) ≈ m1(s), m4(replay) ≈ m4(s)

proof_skeleton.m1: sign(diff) pattern repeats identically in both halves → expectation preserved
proof_skeleton.m4: amplitude distribution unchanged by duplication → transition matrix invariant
proof_skeleton.m2: half_corr depends on first half only → preserved exactly
proof_skeleton.m3: duplication increases redundancy → compression ratio decreases

corollary: Replay variant maps to fixed offset in embedding space → classifier learns as distinct class → 100% separation
